title: Self-Judgment
description: The recognition across multiple traditions that there is no external judge after death — the judgment is the soul's own self-assessment. In the Tibetan tradition, Yama Dharmaraja is a reflection of one's own actions; in Egypt, the heart itself testifies before Osiris; in Steiner, the soul experiences the pain it caused others as its own pain; in Tantraloka, Śiva witnesses the soul's karmic coverings.
